key_insight: A layerwise self-adaptive PINN with dual-phase optimization improves stability and accuracy for high-Reynolds-number multidimensional coupled Burgers' equations by dynamically reweighting physics, initial, and boundary losses.
engineering_relevance: The method offers a mesh-free way to resolve sharp shock fronts in convection-dominated flow problems where traditional finite difference and finite element solvers struggle with stability and grid sensitivity.
method: Layerwise self-adaptive weighting updates loss penalties during training.
Dual-phase optimization is used for more stable convergence.
Benchmarked against standard PINN variants with and without L-BFGS.
evidence_signal: Numerical experiments in MATLAB show lower relative L2 error and better shock-front capture than standard PINNs.
watch_item: Adaptive weighting can be sensitive to training setup and may require careful tuning to avoid imbalance across residual, initial, and boundary terms.
paper_title: MATLAB-Based Layerwise Self-Adaptive Physics-Informed Neural Network in Applications to Multidimensional Coupled Burgers' Equations with High Reynolds Numbers
